title: Asteroids Play Toss with Cosmic Snowballs
summary: Scientists have discovered that asteroids can throw rocks and dust at each other, like playing a slow game of catch in space. This happens in binary asteroid systems, where two asteroids orbit each other. NASA's DART mission took pictures of an asteroid called Dimorphos before crashing into it. In those pictures, researchers saw bright streaks that look like fan shapes. These streaks show that material moved from one asteroid to another, which they call "cosmic snowballs." This helps scientists learn more about asteroids, especially ones that might come near Earth. The study was led by a team from the University of Maryland and published in 2026.

questions.correct_answer: To learn about asteroids near Earth

background_read: Asteroids are rocky objects in space that can be small or large. Some asteroids travel near Earth, and scientists study them to understand if they might pose a danger. NASA's DART mission was a test to see if we could change an asteroid's path by crashing into it. This new discovery shows that asteroids in pairs can exchange material, which helps us learn how they behave over time.
Article_Structure: WHO: Scientists from the University of Maryland and NASA
WHAT: Found that asteroids throw cosmic snowballs at each other
WHERE: In binary asteroid systems in space
WHY: To understand asteroids better and protect Earth

perspectives.description: This shows asteroids are more active than thought, with material moving between them.
